title: HTTP Semantics, Resources, Methods, and Status Contracts · Execution Trace: Resource Representation State
summary: A task service uses POST for every action, mutates state on GET, returns 200 for rejected work, and retries duplicate writes without a resource or method contract. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo resource representation state increment in resource, method, status, and retry semantics table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate resource identity, current server state, selected representation, transfer content, and backing storage model.
Assign startup, request, dependency, shutdown, rollback, alert, and data-recovery evidence to named owners and done conditions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Resource Representation State · Theory Resource Representation State
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 1208 in a privacy-sensitive records API using conditional requests. Build deterministic Go evidence to connect method semantics, resource state, status, and durable effect; then drain the old revision while a canary fails its changed-case invariant. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate resource identity, current server state, selected representation, transfer content, and backing storage model.
